title: SurfaceEvaluator Object
description: Surface evaluator that is obtained from a transient surface and allows you to perform various evaluations on the surface.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: getCurvature
methods.description: Get the curvature values at a parameter positions on the surface.

methods.name: getCurvatures
methods.description: Get the curvature values at a number of parameter positions on the surface.

methods.name: getFirstDerivative
methods.description: Get the first derivative of the surface at the specified parameter position.

methods.name: getFirstDerivatives
methods.description: Get the first derivatives of the surface at the specified parameter positions.

methods.name: getIsoCurve
methods.description: Gets (by extraction) a curve that follows a constant u or v parameter along the surface. The curve will have the same properties as the surface in the direction of the extraction. For example, when a curve is extracted from the periodic direction of a surface, the extracted curve will also be periodic. The type of curve returned is dependent on the shape the surface.   Getting an iso curve is limited to a SurfaceEvaluator that is obtained from a BRepFace. It will fail when the SurfaceEvaluator is obtained from a geometry object (Plane, Sphere, Torus, Cylinder, Cone, EllipticalCone, EllipticalCylinder, or NurbsSurface).

methods.name: getModelCurveFromParametricCurve
methods.description: Creates the 3D equivalent curve in model space, of a 2D curve defined in the parametric space of the surface.

methods.name: getNormalAtParameter
methods.description: Gets the surface normal at a parameter position on the surface.

methods.name: getNormalAtPoint
methods.description: Gets the surface normal at a point on the surface.

methods.name: getNormalsAtParameters
methods.description: Gets the surface normal at a number of parameter positions on the surface.

methods.name: getNormalsAtPoints
methods.description: Gets the surface normal at a number of positions on the surface.

methods.name: getParamAnomaly
methods.description: Gets details about anomalies in parameter space of the surface. This includes information about periodic intervals, singularities, or unbounded parameter ranges.

methods.name: getParameterAtPoint
methods.description: Get the parameter position that correspond to a point on the surface. For reliable results, the point should lie on the surface within model tolerance. If the point does not lie on the surface, the parameter of the nearest point on the surface will generally be returned.

methods.name: getParametersAtPoints
methods.description: Get the parameter positions that correspond to a set of points on the surface. For reliable results, the points should lie on the surface within model tolerance. If the points do not lie on the surface, the parameter of the nearest point on the surface will generally be returned.

methods.name: getPointAtParameter
methods.description: Get the point on the surface that correspond to evaluating a parameter position on the surface.

methods.name: getPointsAtParameters
methods.description: Get the points on the surface that correspond to evaluating a set of parameter positions on the surface.

methods.name: getSecondDerivative
methods.description: Get the second derivative of the surface at the specified parameter position.

methods.name: getSecondDerivatives
methods.description: Get the second derivatives of the surface at the specified parameter positions.

methods.name: getThirdDerivative
methods.description: Get the third derivative of the surface at the specified parameter position.

methods.name: getThirdDerivatives
methods.description: Get the third derivatives of the surface at the specified parameter positions.

methods.name: isParameterOnFace
methods.description: Determines if the specified parameter position lies within the surface. When the SurfaceEvaluator is obtained from a BRepFace object, this will respect the boundaries of the face and return true when point is on the visible portion of the surface. When obtained from surface geometry it returns true if the point is within the parametric range of surface.

methods.name: parametricRange
methods.description: Returns the parametric range of the surface. If the surface is periodic in a direction, the range is set to the principle period's range. If the surface is only upper bounded in a direction, the lower bound is set to -double-max. If the surface is only lower bounded in a direction, the upper bound is set to double-max. If the surface is unbounded in a direction, the lower bound and upper bound of the range will both be zero.

properties.name: area
properties.description: Returns the area of the surface. This is typically used when the SurfaceEvaluator is associated with a BRepFace object where it is always valid. This can fail in the case where the SurfaceEvaluator is associated with one of the geometry classes, (Plane, Cylinder, Cone, EllipticalCone, or EllipticalCylinder object), because these surfaces are unbounded. A BRepFace, even one of these shapes, is bounded by its edges and has a well-defined area.

properties.name: isClosedInU
properties.description: Returns if the surface is closed (forms a loop) in the U direction

properties.name: isClosedInV
properties.description: Returns if the surface is closed (forms a loop) in the V direction

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

area.title: SurfaceEvaluator.area Property
area.description: Returns the area of the surface. This is typically used when the SurfaceEvaluator is associated with a BRepFace object where it is always valid. This can fail in the case where the SurfaceEvaluator is associated with one of the geometry classes, (Plane, Cylinder, Cone, EllipticalCone, or EllipticalCylinder object), because these surfaces are unbounded. A BRepFace, even one of these shapes, is bounded by its edges and has a well-defined area.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = surfaceEvaluator_var.area

isClosedInU.title: SurfaceEvaluator.isClosedInU Property
isClosedInU.description: Returns if the surface is closed (forms a loop) in the U direction

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = surfaceEvaluator_var.isClosedInU

isClosedInV.title: SurfaceEvaluator.isClosedInV Property
isClosedInV.description: Returns if the surface is closed (forms a loop) in the V direction

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = surfaceEvaluator_var.isClosedInV

isValid.title: SurfaceEvaluator.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = surfaceEvaluator_var.isValid

objectType.title: SurfaceEvaluator.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = surfaceEvaluator_var.objectType

classType.title: SurfaceEvaluator.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

getCurvature.title: SurfaceEvaluator.getCurvature Method
getCurvature.description: Get the curvature values at a parameter positions on the surface.
getCurvature.return_description: Returns true if the curvature was successfully returned.

parameters.name: parameter
parameters.description: The parameter positions to return curvature information at.

parameters.name: maxTangent
parameters.description: The output directions of maximum curvature at the position on the surface.

parameters.name: maxCurvature
parameters.description: The output magnitude of the maximum curvature at the position on the surface.

parameters.name: minCurvature
parameters.description: The output magnitude of the minimum curvature at the position on the surface. The minimum curvature direction is perpendicular to the maximum curvature tangent directions.

getCurvatures.title: SurfaceEvaluator.getCurvatures Method
getCurvatures.description: Get the curvature values at a number of parameter positions on the surface.
getCurvatures.return_description: Returns true if the curvatures were successfully returned.

parameters.name: parameters
parameters.description: The array of parameter positions to return curvature information at. Each parameter position must be with the range of the parameter extents as verified by isParameterOnFace.

parameters.name: maxTangents
parameters.description: The output array of directions of maximum curvature at each position on the surface. The length of this array will be the same as the length of the parameters array provided.

parameters.name: maxCurvatures
parameters.description: The output array of the magnitude of the maximum curvature at each position on the surface. The length of this array will be the same as the length of the parameters array provided.

parameters.name: minCurvatures
parameters.description: The output array of the magnitude of the minimum curvature at each position on the surface. The minimum curvature direction is perpendicular to the maximum curvature tangent directions. The length of this array will be the same as the length of the parameters array provided.

getFirstDerivative.title: SurfaceEvaluator.getFirstDerivative Method
getFirstDerivative.description: Get the first derivative of the surface at the specified parameter position.
getFirstDerivative.return_description: Returns true if the first derivative was successfully returned.

parameters.name: parameter
parameters.description: The parameter positions to get the surface first derivative at. The parameter position must be within the range of the parameter extents as verified by isParameterOnFace.

parameters.name: partialU
parameters.description: The output first derivative U partial vector at the parameter position specified.

parameters.name: partialV
parameters.description: The output first derivative V partial vector at the parameter position specified.

getFirstDerivatives.title: SurfaceEvaluator.getFirstDerivatives Method
getFirstDerivatives.description: Get the first derivatives of the surface at the specified parameter positions.
getFirstDerivatives.return_description: Returns true if the first derivatives were successfully returned.

parameters.name: parameters
parameters.description: The array of parameter positions to get the surface first derivative at. Each parameter position must be within the range of the parameter extents as verified by isParameterOnFace.

parameters.name: partialsU
parameters.description: The output array of first derivative U partial vectors at each parameter position specified. The length of this array is equal to the length of the parameters array specified.

parameters.name: partialsV
parameters.description: The output array of first derivative V partial vectors at each parameter position specified. The length of this array is equal to the length of the parameters array specified.

getIsoCurve.title: SurfaceEvaluator.getIsoCurve Method
getIsoCurve.description: Gets (by extraction) a curve that follows a constant u or v parameter along the surface. The curve will have the same properties as the surface in the direction of the extraction. For example, when a curve is extracted from the periodic direction of a surface, the extracted curve will also be periodic. The type of curve returned is dependent on the shape the surface. Getting an iso curve is limited to a SurfaceEvaluator that is obtained from a BRepFace. It will fail when the SurfaceEvaluator is obtained from a geometry object (Plane, Sphere, Torus, Cylinder, Cone, EllipticalCone, EllipticalCylinder, or NurbsSurface).
getIsoCurve.return_description: Returns an ObjectCollection that contains one or more curves. Multiple curves are returned when the SurfaceEvaluator is obtained from a Face and the curve cuts across internal boundaries. The resulting curves are trimmed to the boundaries of the Face. When the SurfaceEvaluator is obtained from a geometry object, a single curve is returned because there are no boundaries to trim the curve. The type of curve(s) returned is dependent on the shape of the surface.

parameters.name: parameter
parameters.description: The parameter at which to extract the curve

parameters.name: isUDirection
parameters.description: A bool that indicates whether to extract the curve from the U or V direction

getModelCurveFromParametricCurve.title: SurfaceEvaluator.getModelCurveFromParametricCurve Method
getModelCurveFromParametricCurve.description: Creates the 3D equivalent curve in model space, of a 2D curve defined in the parametric space of the surface.
getModelCurveFromParametricCurve.return_description: Returns an ObjectCollection containing one or more curves.  When the SufaceEvaluatior is obtained from a face, and the curve cuts across internal  boundaries of the face, multiple curves are returned. The returned curves are trimmed  to the boundaries of the face.  If the SurfaceEvaluator is obtained from a geometry object, a single curve returned  because there are no boundaries with which to trim the curve.  The type of curve(s) returned depends on the shape of the input curve and surface.

parameters.name: parametricCurve
parameters.description: The parameter space curve to map into this surface's parameter space.

getNormalAtParameter.title: SurfaceEvaluator.getNormalAtParameter Method
getNormalAtParameter.description: Gets the surface normal at a parameter position on the surface.
getNormalAtParameter.return_description: Returns true if the normal was successfully returned.

parameters.name: parameter
parameters.description: The parameter position to return the normal at. The parameter position must be with the range of the parameter extents as verified by isParameterOnFace.

parameters.name: normal
parameters.description: The output normal for the parameter position on the surface.

getNormalAtPoint.title: SurfaceEvaluator.getNormalAtPoint Method
getNormalAtPoint.description: Gets the surface normal at a point on the surface.
getNormalAtPoint.return_description: Returns true if the normal was successfully returned.

parameters.name: point
parameters.description: The point to return the normal at. For reliable results the point should lie on the surface.

parameters.name: normal
parameters.description: The output normal for the point on the surface.

getNormalsAtParameters.title: SurfaceEvaluator.getNormalsAtParameters Method
getNormalsAtParameters.description: Gets the surface normal at a number of parameter positions on the surface.
getNormalsAtParameters.return_description: Returns true if the normals were successfully returned.

parameters.name: parameters
parameters.description: The array of parameter positions to return the normal at. Each parameter position must be with the range of the parameter extents as verified by isParameterOnFace.

parameters.name: normals
parameters.description: The output array of normals for each parameter position on the surface. The length of this array will be the same as the length of the parameters array provided.

getNormalsAtPoints.title: SurfaceEvaluator.getNormalsAtPoints Method
getNormalsAtPoints.description: Gets the surface normal at a number of positions on the surface.
getNormalsAtPoints.return_description: Returns true if the normals were successfully returned.

parameters.name: points
parameters.description: The array of points to return the normal at. For reliable results each point should lie on the surface.

parameters.name: normals
parameters.description: The output array of normals for each point on the surface. The length of this array will be the same as the length of the points array provided.

getParamAnomaly.title: SurfaceEvaluator.getParamAnomaly Method
getParamAnomaly.description: Gets details about anomalies in parameter space of the surface. This includes information about periodic intervals, singularities, or unbounded parameter ranges.

syntax.python: (returnValue, periodicityU, periodicityV, singularitiesU, singularitiesV, unboundedParameters) = surfaceEvaluator_var.getParamAnomaly()

getParamAnomaly.return_description: Returns true if the parameter anomalies were successfully returned.

parameters.name: periodicityU
parameters.description: The output array with information about the period of the surface in U. periodicityU[0] will contain the period of the surface in U. If periodicityU[0] is 0, the surface is not periodic in U. If the surface is periodic in U, peridocityU[1] will contain the parameter value at the start of the principle period.

parameters.name: periodicityV
parameters.description: The output array with information about the period of the surface in V. periodicityV[0] will contain the period of the surface in V. If periodicityV[0] is 0, the surface is not periodic in V. If the surface is periodic in V, peridocityV[1] will contain the parameter value at the start of the principle period.

parameters.name: singularitiesU
parameters.description: The output array parameter values of singularities in U. If this array is empty, there are no singularities in U.

parameters.name: singularitiesV
parameters.description: The output array parameter values of singularities in V. If this array is empty, there are no singularities in V.

parameters.name: unboundedParameters
parameters.description: The output array that indicates if the parameter range is unbounded in U or V. unboundedParameters[0] will be true if U is unbounded. unboundedParameters[1] will be true if V is unbounded.

getParameterAtPoint.title: SurfaceEvaluator.getParameterAtPoint Method
getParameterAtPoint.description: Get the parameter position that correspond to a point on the surface. For reliable results, the point should lie on the surface within model tolerance. If the point does not lie on the surface, the parameter of the nearest point on the surface will generally be returned.
getParameterAtPoint.return_description: Returns true of the parameter was successfully returned.

parameters.name: point
parameters.description: The point to get the curve parameter value at.

parameters.name: parameter
parameters.description: The output parameter position corresponding to the point.

getParametersAtPoints.title: SurfaceEvaluator.getParametersAtPoints Method
getParametersAtPoints.description: Get the parameter positions that correspond to a set of points on the surface. For reliable results, the points should lie on the surface within model tolerance. If the points do not lie on the surface, the parameter of the nearest point on the surface will generally be returned.
getParametersAtPoints.return_description: Returns true if the parameters were successfully returned.

parameters.name: points
parameters.description: An array of points to get the surface parameter values at.

parameters.name: parameters
parameters.description: The output array of parameter positions corresponding to the set of points. The length of this array will be equal to the length of the points array specified.

getPointAtParameter.title: SurfaceEvaluator.getPointAtParameter Method
getPointAtParameter.description: Get the point on the surface that correspond to evaluating a parameter position on the surface.
getPointAtParameter.return_description: Returns true if the point was successfully returned.

parameters.name: parameter
parameters.description: The parameter positions to evaluate the surface position at. The parameter position must be within the range of the parameter extents as verified by isParameterOnFace.

parameters.name: point
parameters.description: The output point corresponding to evaluating the curve at that parameter position.

getPointsAtParameters.title: SurfaceEvaluator.getPointsAtParameters Method
getPointsAtParameters.description: Get the points on the surface that correspond to evaluating a set of parameter positions on the surface.
getPointsAtParameters.return_description: Returns true if the points were successfully returned.

parameters.name: parameters
parameters.description: The array of parameter positions to evaluate the surface position at. Each parameter position must be within the range of the parameter extents as verified by isParameterOnFace.

parameters.name: points
parameters.description: The output array of points corresponding to evaluating the curve at that parameter position. The length of this array will be equal to the length of the parameters array specified.

getSecondDerivative.title: SurfaceEvaluator.getSecondDerivative Method
getSecondDerivative.description: Get the second derivative of the surface at the specified parameter position.
getSecondDerivative.return_description: Returns true if the second derivative was successfully returned.

parameters.name: parameter
parameters.description: The parameter position to get the surface second derivative at. The parameter position must be within the range of the parameter extents as verified by isParameterOnFace.

parameters.name: partialUU
parameters.description: The output second derivative UU partial vector at each parameter position specified.

parameters.name: partialUV
parameters.description: The output second derivative UV mixed partial vector at each parameter position specified.

parameters.name: partialVV
parameters.description: The output second derivative VV partial vector at each parameter position specified.

getSecondDerivatives.title: SurfaceEvaluator.getSecondDerivatives Method
getSecondDerivatives.description: Get the second derivatives of the surface at the specified parameter positions.
getSecondDerivatives.return_description: Returns true if the second derivatives were successfully returned.

parameters.name: parameters
parameters.description: The array of parameter positions to get the surface second derivative at. Each parameter position must be within the range of the parameter extents as verified by isParameterOnFace.

parameters.name: partialsUU
parameters.description: The output array of second derivative UU partial vectors at each parameter position specified. The length of this array is equal to the length of the parameters array specified.

parameters.name: partialsUV
parameters.description: The output array of second derivative UV mixed partial vectors at each parameter position specified. The length of this array is equal to the length of the parameters array specified.

parameters.name: partialsVV
parameters.description: The output array of second derivative VV partial vectors at each parameter position specified. The length of this array is equal to the length of the parameters array specified.

getThirdDerivative.title: SurfaceEvaluator.getThirdDerivative Method
getThirdDerivative.description: Get the third derivative of the surface at the specified parameter position.
getThirdDerivative.return_description: Returns true if the third derivative was successfully returned.

parameters.name: parameter
parameters.description: The parameter position to get the surface third derivative at. The parameter position must be within the range of the parameter extents as verified by isParameterOnFace.

parameters.name: partialUUU
parameters.description: The output third derivative UUU partial vector at each parameter position specified.

parameters.name: partialVVV
parameters.description: The output third derivative VVV partial vector at each parameter position specified.

getThirdDerivatives.title: SurfaceEvaluator.getThirdDerivatives Method
getThirdDerivatives.description: Get the third derivatives of the surface at the specified parameter positions.
getThirdDerivatives.return_description: Returns true if the third derivatives were successfully returned.

parameters.name: parameters
parameters.description: The array of parameter positions to get the surface third derivative at. Each parameter position must be within the range of the parameter extents as verified by isParameterOnFace.

parameters.name: partialsUUU
parameters.description: The output array of third derivative UUU partial vectors at each parameter position specified. The length of this array is equal to the length of the parameters array specified.

parameters.name: partialsVVV
parameters.description: The output array of third derivative VVV partial vectors at each parameter position specified. The length of this array is equal to the length of the parameters array specified.

isParameterOnFace.title: SurfaceEvaluator.isParameterOnFace Method
isParameterOnFace.description: Determines if the specified parameter position lies within the surface. When the SurfaceEvaluator is obtained from a BRepFace object, this will respect the boundaries of the face and return true when point is on the visible portion of the surface. When obtained from surface geometry it returns true if the point is within the parametric range of surface.
isParameterOnFace.return_description: Returns true if the parameter position lies within the surface.

parameters.name: parameter
parameters.description: The parameter position to test.

parametricRange.title: SurfaceEvaluator.parametricRange Method
parametricRange.description: Returns the parametric range of the surface. If the surface is periodic in a direction, the range is set to the principle period's range. If the surface is only upper bounded in a direction, the lower bound is set to -double-max. If the surface is only lower bounded in a direction, the upper bound is set to double-max. If the surface is unbounded in a direction, the lower bound and upper bound of the range will both be zero.
parametricRange.return_description: Returns the bounding box with the parameter extents, with the X value being the U range, and the Y value being the V range.
